transliteration:
amānitvam adambhitvam ahiṁsā kṣāntir ārjavam |
ācāryopāsanaṁ śaucaṁ sthairyam ātmavini-grahaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Humility, pridelessness, non-violence, tolerance, straightforwardness; service to the teacher, purity, steadfastness, self-restraint.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the first eight cognitive-affective virtues that dismantle the egoic field (kṣetra). They are intra-psychic hygiene: lowering self-inflation, cooling aggression, stabilizing attention, and installing an internal observer.
psychological_state: Secure-functioning mindset: low narcissistic defenses, high distress-tolerance, and prefrontal governance over amygdala reactivity.
trigger_scenario: You’ve just been publicly corrected by your supervisor on Zoom; your first impulse is to defend or belittle, but you pause, breathe, and respond with curiosity—an instant of amānitvam & kṣānti.
anchor_text: Live gently, think clearly, serve humbly, and watch the ego quietly dissolve.
